title: Flavorful Crispy Malaysian Duck Satay
description: A kid-friendly Malaysian dish featuring duck fat, with a briny and cheese-pull flavor profile, sugar-free friendly and perfect for a weeknight dinner
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together coconut milk, soy sauce, lime juice, garlic, ginger, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the duck breast strips to the marinade and mix well to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat, then thread the marinated duck strips onto the bamboo skewers.
Brush the grill or grill pan with peanut oil to prevent sticking, then grill the satay for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
In a small saucepan, melt the duck fat over low heat, then brush the melted fat onto the grilled satay.
Serve the satay hot with your favorite dipping sauce, such as peanut sauce or kecap manis.
Garnish with sliced lime wedges and chopped cilantro, if desired.
Repeat with remaining ingredients, if necessary.
Let the satay rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute.
